How fresh is this? Full rebuild end-of-day after the close on the 2026-07-23 data, then refreshed hourly during US market hours (prices, intraday options-flow signals; the AI reads stay on the EOD batch). Stock & technical screeners update once a day (EOD), options-flow screeners (Intraday ~30 min) refresh during the session. Insider feeds are daily, 13F is quarterly. Each card shows its own refresh cadence and data date. Screeners are ordered highest data confidence first.

★ Cross-screener confluence

CONFLUENCE
🧠 Flags tickers appearing across 2 or more coherent screeners within one trading day, amplifying signal quality by requiring independent indicator agreement.
Backtested combos first (May-2026 sweeps + pairs battery), then today's coherent overlaps. Trade-ready funnel: best_trades_today →
Validated & debunked combos — numbers, not vibes
ComboData confidenceBacktestRead

Any 3+ VALID screeners within ±1 dayFULL SIZEWR 81.7% · Sharpe 2.68 · p_bonf < 0.0001 (N=126, bucket-level)The bucket edge: triple agreement among validated screeners beats solos by +5.8pp WR.

Generic confluence carries the bucket-level stats only (3+ VALID screeners: WR 81.7% at +20d). A pair NOT listed above has no proven pair-level edge - treat the overlap as attention, not size. New-screener pairs re-enter the battery as their history grows.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

MS tops the batch at 157 on Clenow momentum, a pullback entry, and variance premium - a trend-plus-mean-reversion setup with vol edge, and the cleanest screener stack in the cohort. AMBP prints 143 across four screeners including block traders and PEAD gap-up, the widest screener footprint today, though the mix is more heterogeneous than MS. LLY mirrors MS's exact three-screener combination at 138, giving that Clenow/pullback/VRP pattern three appearances, which is worth registering. Six of the ten names land at exactly 100, the floor threshold, so the score distribution is top-heavy rather than broad - this is three names and a long tail. Critically, matched_combo is null across the entire batch, meaning no pick triggers a VALID validated combo, which removes the highest-probability edge cases from the table on a NEUTRAL macro tape (regime score 55.03); MS is the first look at moderate conviction only.

🎈 Blow-off top detector NEW

BLOWOFF_DETECTOR
🧠 Spots parabolic tops about to roll over on the top-sector winners — Greenwood-Shleifer-You 2-year rallies, Edwards-Magee parabolic, gap exhaustion, island reversals and LPPLS variance acceleration. Only the max-drawdown-backtested signals carry weight; the academic heuristics confirm. GO / NO GO on naked puts comes from the live Polygon options chain.

Read
Top blow-off candidate today: MPC (score 10, target ~$276.20 (DD20d -11.6%)).

3 ticker(s) fire at least ONE Bonferroni-validated backtested signal (25y, 50,109 obs, p<0.001). 2 stack ≥3 points = high conviction.

Methodology: the backtest-validated signals measure max drawdown over 20d (not the mean forward return — masked by rebounds in a bull market). The academic heuristics (Greenwood-Shleifer-You, Edwards-Magee, gap, island, LPPLS) stay displayed for context but do not survive Bonferroni on their own — they only confirm.
How to read this
Backtest-validated signals (25 years, 91 tickers, 50,109 observations, p<0.001):
• Two-red parabolic (weight 3) — 2 red days after parabolic. DD 20d -11.55%, lift +20pp, N=302.
• Stoch cross<80 + parabolic (weight 2) — DD 20d -9.65%, N=148.
• Rally + red on volume (weight 2) — rally 10%+ then red day. DD 20d -7.23%, N=93.
• Shooting star / BB fail / Bearish engulf (weight 1) — DD 20d -3 to -5%.

Options decision = multi-factor composite 0-100 (IV Rank · IV-HV · term struct · skew 25Δ · implied/DD ratio · liquidity). Score ≥70 + liquidity OK → 🟢 GO ATM Put. Score 50-70 → 🟡 GO LIGHT OTM 25Δ. <50 or liquidity KO → 🔴 NO GO + reason.

Global composite REJECT on its own (mean forward return bull-biased). Individual signals VALID on the max-DD metric.

Data confidence FULL SIZE · Sharpe 2.39 [CI 1.48-3.33] · N=273 · WR 68% · PF 2.20 · p(Bonf) 0.000 · ½-Kelly 0.18 Full methodology →
🧠 Detects institutional intent via unusual options sweeps confirmed by next-day OI delta, EMA8/21 alignment, and low-OI anomalies to isolate informed flow.
Construction
Unusual options sweep + next-day OI delta confirmation (OPENING vs CLOSING) + EMA8/21 structure + low-OI anomaly. Score includes OI Delta, premium $, signal persistence, EMA alignment.
Top picks: $XYZ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1XYZ ↑50 Watch$76.49 -1.25%BULLISH SWEEPCALL83$240KOPENING113958WEAK22
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Starts from the prior day's UNUSUAL signals, then checks T+1 OI Delta
  • OI Delta: OPENING (+25), LIKELY_OPENING (+15), CLOSING (-50), NEUTRAL (+5)
  • 20d flow persistence: net≥5 → +30, ≥3 → +20, ≥2 → +10, ≤-2 → -15; streak bonus ≥3
  • Multi-leg spread detected: -30; Low-OI anomaly: +15
  • EMA STRONG +10 / PULLBACK +5 / BROKEN -10; quality HIGH +5 / LOW -5 (min mcap $500M)
  • Archives only non-CLOSING signals AND score ≥ 50
Score
clamp(0,100, OI_delta + persistence + spread + low_oi + ema + quality). CLOSING dropped. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking has 1 resolved trade out of 223 signals with tb_expired_pct at zero, making the reported 100% win rate and 200% avg return statistically meaningless - the backtest's 68.1% win rate cannot be confirmed or contradicted until at least 20 trades close out.

Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 222 closed · WR TB 100% · 0% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is a single signal - XYZ at score 50, Watch tier, flagged as a bullish sweep long. That score clears neither the 65 nor the 80 threshold, placing it in the lowest conviction bucket the screener produces. Score distribution is as thin as it gets: n_signals at 1, zero names above 65, median and max both pinned at 50. Macro sits neutral at 55.03, offering no directional tailwind to push a marginal setup over the line. This is a weak day, and XYZ at Watch-tier warrants at most a small pilot to track flow persistence, not a conviction trade.

🧠 Ranks premium-selling candidates by IV30-minus-HV30 spread, adjusted for Sinclair sector bias and value factors, targeting structurally overpaid implied volatility.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Liquid universe: market cap ≥ $2B, volume ≥ 500K
  • VP spread (IV Rank vs HV30) - weight 35%
  • Sinclair sector bias (Utilities +31% … Tech -8%) - weight 25%
  • Value factors (P/E, RoE, RoA, D/E, mcap) - weight 25%; IV Rank - weight 15%
  • Garbage-stock bonus (high P/E, low RoE) up to +15
Score
clamp(0,100, vp×0.35 + sector×0.25 + value×0.25 + ivr×0.15 + garbage_bonus). LONG (sell puts), top 30.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

PCG and EXC lead at 76, tied atop a batch dominated by utilities and staples, with DUK close behind at 75. The Sinclair framework assigns a +31% structural bias to utilities, so this concentration is methodologically expected, not a coincidence. Score distribution is compressed: max 76, median 70, zero names above 80, and 34 of 38 signals above 65, a tight cluster with no breakout score anywhere. The NEUTRAL macro regime (55.03) is workable for premium selling but provides no directional tailwind. PCG first at moderate conviction, sharing the top score in the sector the model explicitly favors.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads the variance_premium_daily table on the latest day
  • Keeps only the MID tertile (OOS sweet spot: neither LOW cheap nor HIGH catalyst-priced)
  • Sorted by vrp_proxy ascending, top 30
Score
max(50, 100 − rank×50/n) within the MID tertile. LONG.
🧠 AI read · today's batch

AMDY at a perfect score of 100 and OMC at 99 lead today's batch, both rated Very strong, though null pattern data on every name prevents a clean mechanistic thesis on any of them. SNEX at 97 completes the top three with the same information gap. The distribution is top-heavy: 13 of 30 signals score 80 or above and the median sits at 75, so quality concentration is real even if individual setups lack pattern detail. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 55.03 is neither a tailwind nor a headwind for VRP_MID, which targets orderly vol pricing rather than directional regime exposure. Start with AMDY given the perfect score, but cap size at half-weight until pattern data populates.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • For each ticker on the latest options_daily_flow day
  • Trigger: put_volume ≥ 3.0× the 30d moving average of put_volume
  • AND put_volume ≥ 100 contracts
Score
min(100, put_ratio × 20) where put_ratio = put_volume / 30d average. LONG (contrarian: hedging = bottom near).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

Score distribution is fully compressed: 50 signals, median 100, 47 of 50 scoring 80 or above, meaning the screener provides no relative ranking within today's batch. All eight picks (ABG, ADTN, AIRJ, AMLX, AMP, ARWR, AWI, BOX) score 100 with no pattern data attached, so differentiation has to come from outside the signal itself. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 55.03 neither supports nor undermines the Kerberos thesis, which requires calm tape rather than directional momentum. AMP is the first look at moderate conviction: it is the most liquid and institutionally-covered name in the group, but ceiling compression across the board limits confidence in any single name.

Construction

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Excluded if pulled back > 25% below the 52-week high
  • Uptrend: 90d slope > +0.20%/day, 60d slope > +0.25%/day, 90d R² > 0.70, price > EMA50 and EMA200
  • Pullback in progress: 30d slope < +0.15%/day, 10d slope < +0.05%/day, RSI(14) between 40 and 65 (sweet spot 45-55)
  • Healthy pullback: 10d/30d volume < 0.80, ATR contracting, no down gaps, Fibonacci level 38/50/62%, <15% below 52w high
  • Recovery: higher lows, rising 5d volume, bullish RSI divergence
Score
Uptrend (max 30) + Pullback (max 25) + Healthy (max 25) + Recovery (max 30). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day track contradicts the backtest directly - 44% win rate and -3.8% average vs. SPY +1.9% earns a KILL verdict against the backtest's 65% win rate, though monthly cohorts have recovered from February's trough of 24.6% to 60% in June, a partial rehabilitation that has not yet overturned the verdict.
📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
STARTER ONLYWR 44% · avg -3.8% vs SPY +1.9% (-5.7 pts)
Last 90 days: 620 signals captured · 491 resolved at +20d · WR 44% · avg -3.8% · vs SPY +1.9%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 493 closed · WR TB 34% · 17% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

DHC leads at a perfect score of 100, the only name at that level, while ARKO and WDC anchor a six-name cluster at 90, all printing the LAUNCH pattern. The score distribution is extreme: all 50 signals clear 80 with a median of 86, leaving no gradient to differentiate the batch beyond DHC's lone peak. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 55.03 neither blocks nor supports the setup. The critical problem is that 'Very strong' tier names in 90-day live tracking have averaged -6.7% with a 35% win rate, making today's eight 'Very strong' picks structurally suspect going in. DHC first on score if forced to act, but conviction is low.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

CTMX and QBTS are the standouts at 94, both Very strong tier and the highest scores in today's batch of 34. PRCT (91) and CELH (90) round out the Very strong cluster. Score distribution is unusually compressed: all 34 signals clear the 80 threshold, median sits at 86, and every name lands in the top two scoring bands, so today is a top-heavy batch with no weak-signal dilution dragging the basket. Macro regime is NEUTRAL at 55.03, which provides no mean-reversion tailwind but does not actively oppose a contrarian setup. Start with CTMX at moderate conviction given the tied-for-top score, and spread across the four Very strong names rather than concentrating; this is a basket trade, not a single-name trigger.

Data confidence FULL SIZE VVIX climax V3 STRICT_VALID, P(VIX spike) 11%->4% in 30d, wired prod (lib/vvix_climax_detector) Full methodology →
🧠 Identifies vol-of-vol climax via VVIX percentile, VIX term structure, FOMC proximity and weekend effect to time sell-vol entries at peak fear.
Construction
VVIX percentile + VIX term structure (VIX3M/VIX) + FOMC proximity + weekend effect. Detects 'vol-of-vol' climax (sell-vol opportunities at the top of fear).
Top picks: $SPY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayVerdictComposite
1SPY ↑60 Watch$738.18 -1.23%SELL VOL1.25
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market-level signal archived under SPY (Sinclair composite)
  • VVIX: ≥90th pctile → SELL VOL (+2), ≤10th → BUY VOL (-2)
  • Term structure VIX3M/VIX < 1.0 contango → +1; VIX regime 13-19 sweet +1, >24 danger -1
  • Weekend (Friday) +0.5; FOMC 3d ahead +0.5; Hurst/timing optional
Score
sum of sub-scores (~-6 to +6). ≥3.5 STRONG SELL VOL … ≤-3.5 STRONG BUY VOL. Archived score = clamp(0,100,(total+6)/12×100).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Per-contract filters: Vol/OI > 3.0×, volume > 500, premium > $100K, contract OI ≥ 10, chain OI ≥ 5000
  • Expiration 7-365 DTE; deep ITM excluded
  • Pattern: delta>0.7 → HEDGE, call → BULLISH SWEEP, put → BEARISH SWEEP
  • Timing: LATE (-15) if chasing a >3% move, EARLY (+10)
  • V3 hard filters (before archive): volume < 1.5× the 20d average, |price-EMA20| < 5%, timing ≠ LATE
Score
Vol/OI (max 30) + log premium (max 30) + OTM (max 20) + delta (max 20) + timing (-15/+10) + IV compression (IVRank<15 +15). Archived if ≥50.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eighteen signals with a median score of 58 and only one name above 80 - this is a thin, low-conviction batch. SMH leads at 81 (Strong) but its bearish sweep against a LONG direction tag is an internal contradiction that cuts conviction sharply. SOXX at 70 and GDX at 68 both carry bullish sweeps aligned with their LONG direction, making them the cleaner reads in a neutral macro environment (regime score 55). Score distribution is pedestrian - four names at 65 or above and the rest clustered in Watch territory, which fits a directionless NEUTRAL tape. Prefer SOXX first at low conviction, with GDX as a secondary given gold flow often runs independently of semis.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The two names clearing 80-plus per score_stats do not appear in this pick list, which is the most notable feature of today's batch and needs explaining before any trade is sized. Among names shown, XLP and XLC lead at 60 with no pattern data on either. Five names park at exactly 50, making the distribution floor-heavy and top-light, with only 2 of 11 clearing the 65 threshold. NEUTRAL macro at 55.03 matches the 55 median precisely and offers no directional tailwind. XLP gets marginal preference over XLC in a NEUTRAL tape for its defensive character, but this batch is weak and Watch-tier across the board.

🧠 Detects deep-oversold names (>=40% off 52-week high) with capitulation volume and a reversal candle, betting ATR contraction plus a volume spike marks exhausted selling.
Construction
Deep oversold (≥-40% from 52w high) with capitulation volume + reversal candle. Score combines drawdown depth, ATR contraction, volume spike, and proximity to 52w low. ATR-based stop 2.5×, target 3.0×, max hold 15 days.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Downtrend: 90d slope < -0.20%/day, 60d slope < -0.25%/day, accelerating decline
  • Oversold: RSI(14) < 30, price < -15% below EMA20, < -30% below EMA50, Bollinger %B < 0, <20% above 52w low
  • Capitulation: 10d/30d volume > 1.50, ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 red days out of 10, ≥2 down gaps, reversal bar
  • Signs of Strength: slope deceleration, higher lows, 1-5 day bounce off the low, bullish RSI divergence
  • Persistence bonus: signal seen ≥10 times in 15d = +20, ≥5 times = +10
Score
Downtrend (max 30) + Oversold (max 25) + Capitulation (max 45) + SOS (max 35) + Persistence (0/10/20). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 Live tracking at 57.6% WR and +3.1% avg versus SPY's +1.6% broadly confirms the backtest's positive direction, with the +1.5-point SPY beat as the key number, though January's 29.3% WR and -12.9% avg cohort is a standing reminder that this screener turns ugly fast when regime support disappears.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

NAUT prints a perfect 100 and stands as today's clearest entry, with LENZ and PRIM both at 95 rounding out the top of an unusually concentrated batch where all eight Very strong names carry the same breakout pattern. The score distribution is top-heavy: 29 of 50 signals score 80 or above, median sits at 80, and every signal clears 65, leaving no mid-tier names to differentiate. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 55 adds no fuel but does not block mean-reversion setups either. The most important live-tracking nuance is that Very strong names have averaged only +1.46% over 20 days versus the Signal tier's +5.52%, so the top tier carries a label that the return data does not support. Take NAUT first at moderate conviction, and do not size up purely on tier designation.

Data confidence HALF SIZE · Sharpe 0.73 Stocks-on-the-Move, walk-forward Sharpe 0.73 (+45.6% vs SPY); trend-quality factor, not a per-trade trigger Full methodology →
🧠 Scores stocks on annualized 90-day regression slope times R-squared, entering only when price clears MA100 and SPY is above MA200.
Construction
Andreas Clenow 'Stocks on the Move' (Trading Evolved ch.12). Score = annualized exponential-regression slope (90d) × R², filtered on price > MA100 and max recent gap < 15%. Buys only when SPY > MA200 (regime gate). ATR-based sizing (10 bps risk/position).

🧠 Detects post-earnings price continuation in stocks with positive EPS surprise, gap-up open, and gap-hold through 1-3 weeks, amplified by analyst revision activity.
Construction
Post-earnings continuation: positive EPS surprise + gap up + holds gap into 1-3 weeks. Score uses surprise magnitude, gap size, post-gap action, and analyst revision activity.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • POSITIVE direction (EPS beat), surprise ≥ 5.0%
  • Days since earnings in [1, 20]
  • Consistency (beats in the last 4 quarters) ≥ 2
  • Price ≥ $5, 20d avg volume ≥ 500K; final inclusion if score ≥ 45
Score
surprise (≥20 +40, ≥10 +30, ≥5 +20) + consistency (≥4 +25, ≥3 +15, ≥2 +8) + window (≤3d +20, ≤7 +15, ≤14 +10) + drift (>3% +15, >0 +10). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (55.3% WR, avg +3.46% beating SPY +0.37% by 3.1 points, verdict KEEP) confirms the screener carries real edge beyond its MARGINAL backtest rating, but the June 2026 cohort (37% WR, avg -0.65%) is the key number - a sharp deterioration from May's 59.8% WR that signals the most recent tape has gone soft and warrants sizing down until the trend stabilizes.

GOOG and GOOGL both score 100 and COF scores 95, all three landing in the Very strong tier. The problem is that live tracking shows Very strong is the worst-performing tier, posting 30% win rate and -11.87% average return over 90 days. Macro sits at NEUTRAL (55.03), offering no directional tailwind to lean on. Conviction is low across the board; if forced to rank, COF at least avoids the dual-share redundancy of GOOG and GOOGL, but the KILL verdict on this screener makes any position here a low-confidence trade.

Data confidence HALF SIZE backtest_murphy_intermarket doctrine 8-step: 13 buckets MARGINAL_INVERSE Bonferroni-significant (+6 to +14% SPY at 60-120d after intermarket correlation breaks) Full methodology →
🧠 Fires a contrarian SPY LONG when two or more intermarket pairs show a 2-sigma correlation break over 7 days, betting on regime mean reversion.
Construction
Counts intermarket correlation breaks (|z| >= 2 sigma over 7d) across major pairs from intermarket_corr_history; >= 2 broken pairs fires a contrarian BUY on SPY, severity scales with the count.

Large options block trades (>$200K premium, 1-10 DTE, OTM) on quiet stocks. Confirms next-day with OI delta to verify NEW position vs closing. Score weights total premium, vol/OI ratio, OI confirmation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Stealth institutional accumulation over 60d of OHLCV (optionable universe)
  • Block day: dollar-volume z-score ≥ 1.5σ vs the 60d baseline (excluding the last 5 days)
  • Stealth: low price impact = low |return| / σ_returns
  • Persistence: number of the last 5 days with dollar-volume z > 1.0; up-day bonus
Score
clamp(vol_z/4)×40 + stealth×30 + (persist_5d/5)×20 + (10 if up_day). Archived if ≥50. LONG.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Polygon universe: ≥ 260d history, avg dollar-volume ≥ $200K, price ≥ $1
  • Entry: daily close crosses above the daily EMA(200)
  • Mandatory weekly filter: weekly close > weekly EMA(50)
  • Window: cross within the last 5 trading days
Score
60 + freshness×30 (= (5−bars since cross)/5) + 10 if weekly filter active, capped at 100. LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Every one of today's 50 signals scores 100 and lands in the Very strong tier, a hard ceiling-cluster that gives you no internal ranking to work from. AGCO, BLK, and BKR stand out on liquidity and sector breadth - agriculture equipment, asset management, and oilfield services - but all three carry null on pattern detail, so setup quality beyond the cross itself is unavailable. The distribution is maximally top-heavy, which is structurally positive given the Very strong tier's 63.5% historical win rate, but it makes today's selection discretionary rather than ranked. Macro sits NEUTRAL at 55.03, neither a tailwind nor a headwind for a long-bias batch. BLK is the first name to work given options liquidity and sector leadership, at moderate conviction.

🧠 Flags heavily shorted stocks where borrowing costs spike and price breaks above a key moving average, pressuring shorts to cover.
Construction
High short interest (>20% float) + cost-to-borrow spike + technical breakout above key MA. Score blends SI %, days-to-cover, CTB, and price momentum.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 300K, market cap $200M-$50B
  • Mandatory gate: short interest ≥ 10% of float AND days-to-cover ≥ 3.0
  • Short interest: ≥30% float (+40), ≥20% (+30), ≥15% (+20)
  • Days to cover: ≥8d (+25), ≥5d (+15), ≥3d (+8)
  • Catalyst (not a falling knife): price > SMA50 AND 20d return > +5% (+20)
  • Volume building 10d/30d > 1.3 (+10), RSI(14) 40-70 not extended (+5)
Score
sum of components 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥45. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms rather than contradicts the anecdotal case, with a 58% win rate and +1.6% average return beating SPY's +0.3% by 1.3 points and cohorts recovering from April's -0.7% trough to June's +4.1%, but the key number is the tier split: Strong at 73% wins and +8.3% avg means today's score-80 Strong names sit in the right bucket, while Very strong's 11% win rate and -15.2% avg is a landmine to file away for any future batch where top-scored names roll into that label.

Macro is NEUTRAL at 55 - neither supportive nor damaging for an idiosyncratic squeeze setup, since forced covering is more calendar and float-driven than macro-driven. 🧠 Ranks stocks by relative volume (10/30/90d averages) times multi-horizon price momentum (1/5/20/90d), filtering for positive 20-day trend above $5.
Construction
Reproduces layer #5 of the @kerberos007 thread ('Volume leader screen from RUT-1000'): stocks ranked by relative volume (V/10d, V/30d, V/90d avg) × multi-horizon price momentum (1d/5d/20d/90d). Filters 20d momentum > 0 and price > 5. Score 0-100 = percentile rank of the leader score across the eligible universe.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

DGX, MEDP, and WEX each hit the score ceiling at 100, making them the top three on pure volume-times-momentum rank, though pattern fields are null for every name in today's batch, so there is no catalyst or setup context to lean on. The score distribution is fully compressed: all 50 signals score 80 or above, median 95, meaning the screener offers zero internal differentiation today and the entire output is one undifferentiated cluster. A NEUTRAL macro regime (55.03) neither supports nor undercuts momentum names, but the live tracking verdict is KILL, and that overrides screener rank. If forced to pick one, DGX first, minimum size, with the understanding that this is a surveillance position only until the live edge turns positive.

🧠 Identifies post-earnings gap-up stocks forming a weekly red-candle pullback within 4 weeks, then closing above the prior week's high as a continuation entry.
Construction
Weekly red-candle pullback within first 4 weeks post-earnings gap, then weekly close above prior week's high. Identifies high-quality continuation entries on positive earnings reactions.

🧠 Detects Market Profile excess reversals at value-area extremes where rejection tails exceed 1.8x ATR, the only configuration with a positive backtest edge.
Construction
Market Profile on daily bars (Dalton, Mind Over Markets): rejection-tail bars >= 1.8x ATR formed at value-area extremes (location <= 30% or >= 70%), the only configuration that backtested positive.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) formed in the last 3 bars
  • LONG direction: excess on the LOW side AND trade location ≤ 30%
  • Universe: last_close > 0, max 500 tickers
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). LONG.

🧠 Spots excess supply at value-area highs via a 1.8x ATR rejection tail and location above 70%, targeting downside mean reversion from failed auctions.
Construction
Mirror of DALTON: excess high bars at the top of the value area, rejection tail >= 1.8x ATR, location >= 70%.
Top picks: $OTLY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySideTail ATRLoc %
1OTLY ↓100●●● Very strong$12.56 +16.51%HIGH2.3593.0%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Excess bar detected (wick ≥ 1.8×ATR14) in the last 3 bars
  • SHORT direction: excess on the HIGH side AND trade location ≥ 70%
Score
tail_atr × 50 (tail_atr = wick / ATR14). SHORT.

🧠 Detects clean smart-flow entries by skipping any signal where KVO or Force Index is in flux within a three-day window.
Construction
SMART_FLOW signals filtered by skipping any trade with KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS or FORCE_INDEX_FLIP active ±3 days. Volume rules act as a negative filter (combo OR-skip).
Top picks: $XYZ
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayPatternTypeStrikePremiumOI verdictOI ΔPersistEMAStreakNet 20d
1XYZ ↑50 Watch$76.49 -1.25%BULLISH SWEEPCALL84$204KOPENING110858STRONG22
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Reads SMART_FLOW signals (2-day lookback)
  • Negative filter: SKIP if KVO_SIGNAL_CROSS OR FORCE_INDEX_FLIP volume rule active ±3 trading days
  • T+1 OI Delta filter: SKIP if call_volume(T+1)/call_volume(T) < 0.50
  • Keeps only signals that pass both filters
Score
original SMART_FLOW score, unchanged (no re-scoring). LONG.

The distribution is a single data point at 50, with zero names clearing 65 and zero clearing 80, so there is no top-heavy cluster to trade into. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 55.03 adds no directional tailwind to an already marginal setup. If forced, XYZ is the only name available, but a Watch/50 read calls for minimum size and patience until the screener delivers a denser, higher-scoring batch.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • FOMO momentum required: 5d perf ≥ +3.0% AND 4-week perf ≥ +8.0%
  • Confirmed breakout: close > 2% above resistance (60d high) + day volume > 1.5× the 20d average (CONFIRMED +20, else WEAK +5)
  • Regime gate: not archived if VIX > 20 (OOS decay -0.49 Sharpe at VIX 20-25)
Score
perf_5d×0.3 + perf_4w×0.3 + perf_13w×0.2 + return_1y×0.2 + breakout_points (+20/+5). Not bounded 0-100 → shown here rescaled (top pick = 100). LONG.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

FBRX (score 100, Very strong) and STTK (score 93, Very strong) sit at the top, but the Very strong tier has been the live tape's worst performer at a 26.4% win rate and avg return of -16.6% over 90 days, so the headline scores are noise not signal. MXL (score 79, Signal) is the most relevant name, as the Signal tier carries a 61.5% win rate and avg +10.4% in live tracking despite a sample of only 13 trades. The score distribution is top-heavy and thin, with a median of 46 and only 5 names clearing 80, and all pattern fields are null, limiting structural read. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 55.03 provides no incremental tailwind for breakout momentum. Take MXL first at low conviction and skip the Very strong tier entirely until the live win rate reverses.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • Parabolic trend: 90d slope > +0.25%/day, acceleration (30d > 60d > 90d)
  • Over-extension: RSI(14) > 70, price > +15% above EMA20, > +30% above EMA50, Bollinger %B > 1.0
  • Exhaustion: volume divergence (price up, volume down), ATR ratio > 1.5, ≥7 green days out of 10, ≥2 up gaps
  • Signs of Weakness: 1-5 day rollover off the high, bearish RSI divergence
Score
Trend (max 30) + Extension (max 25) + Exhaustion (max 40) + SOW (max 35). Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. SHORT.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day live track confirms the backtest REJECT rather than contradicting it: a WR of 50.2% and average return of -2.3% versus SPY +1.4% produces -3.7 percentage points of underperformance, and June 2026's cohort (46.3% WR, -2.0% avg on 4,468 trades) makes clear the March 2026 spike - already flagged as an artefact at p=0.084 - was not the start of a real edge.

🧠 Screens for weekly RSI(14) below 30, then scores daily oversold crossover and candlestick reversal signals for mean-reversion long entries.
Construction
Contrarian RSI(14) reversal: HARD GATE weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (coded 2026-06-09), then daily RSI oversold/crossover + candlestick reversal + demethod zone scoring.
Top picks: $ABTC $NFLX $ASR

7ENLV ↑50 Watch$4.36 -4.91%6.8813.64NOENGULFING

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $5, avg volume > 500K, market cap > $100M
  • HARD GATE: weekly RSI(14) < 30 on the last completed week (fail-closed, added 2026-06-09)
  • RSI oversold: RSI(14) ≤ 30 (+15), ≤ 20 extreme (+10), ≥4 days under 30 (+5)
  • Reversal candlestick (hammer/doji/piercing/engulfing) on T-0 to T-2
  • RSI crossover: RSI back above 30 today or yesterday
  • Demethod zone: swing-low cluster (3% tol) + proximity to EMA20 (<3% +5) or EMA50 (+3)
  • Volume spike ≥2.0× (+5) + VIX %B regime (BTFD_BOOST +15 to DANGER -10)
Score
RSI Oversold (max 30) + Candlestick (max 25) + Crossover (max 20) + Demethod+EMA (max 15) + Volume (max 10) + Regime (-10 to +15), bounded 0-100. Unified scale: ≥65 Signal · ≥80 Strong · ≥90 Very strong (below 65 = Watch). Archived if ≥50. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90d tape earns a KEEP - 55% WR and +3.3% avg beating SPY by 2.2 points - but monthly cohorts tell a deteriorating story, with WR decaying from 78.8% in March to 53.2% in June and May average returns going negative at -0.44%, that May number being the key flag that live edge is compressing faster than the headline verdict implies.

ABTC and NFLX share the top score of 58, with ASR next at 55, but all eight names sit in Watch tier and nothing clears the 65 threshold. The score distribution is tight and low, running from 50 to 58 with a median of 53, making this a compressed, uninspiring batch. NEUTRAL macro at 55.03 is neither a tailwind nor a headwind, but the backtest is doctrine-rejected with N=8 and no valid Sharpe, which caps conviction on any name regardless of score. If forced to act, NFLX at 58 earns the first look on liquidity grounds alone, but size small given the batch quality and the total absence of Signal or Strong tier confirmation.

🧠 Flags unusual flow volume and OI delta on crypto-linked ETFs, weighted by spot crypto correlation, to surface informed positioning shifts.
Construction
Tracks unusual flow on crypto-linked ETFs (BITO, IBIT, FBTC, ETHE, GBTC, etc.) - volume vs mean, premium %, OI delta. Score weights flow intensity + spot crypto correlation.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Fixed 12-ticker universe: spot ETFs (IBIT/FBTC/ETHE/BITO) + equity proxies (MSTR/COIN/MARA/RIOT/…)
  • Filters: price > $2, 21d median USD ADV > $5M
  • Uptrend gate: SMA50 > SMA200 AND price > SMA50
  • Pullback: price between -8% and +1% vs SMA20; RSI(14) 35-55; RS vs IBIT 20d > 0
Score
Uptrend (max 35) + Pullback (max 25) + RSI (max 20) + RS (max 10) + Volume (max 10) ± regime, bounded 0-100. Archived if ≥35. LONG.

📊 Recent tracking (last 90 days, ticker by ticker)
FULL SIZE OKWR 65% · avg +7.9% · beats SPY -0.9% by +8.8 pts
Last 90 days: 136 signals captured · 131 resolved at +20d · WR 65% · avg +7.9% · vs SPY -0.9%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 136 closed · WR TB 37% · 35% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Eight names print at score 100 but pattern fields are null across all of them, collapsing differentiation to fundamentals and volatility profile. SRTY is the most structurally notable pick - a LONG flag on a 3x inverse Russell 2000 ETF signals defensive or counter-trend positioning, not risk-on momentum, and stands apart from every other name in today's batch. CELH and BL are the two risk-on names worth isolating given they carry more single-stock return potential than HDV, an income ETF that dilutes the batch's character. Score distribution is top-heavy with 41 of 50 signals at 65 or above and a median of 70, so today is not a thin read. NEUTRAL macro (55.03) neither amplifies nor suppresses the setup, and the SRTY flag warrants caution before leaning hard into the long side - start with CELH at low-to-moderate conviction.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

JEF, FINS, and COE all print 100, the ceiling score, making them the three highest-ranked names in the batch, though pattern data is null across every pick so the setup type is unclassified for all of them. SVRE and TYG follow at 99, with LNBIX at 98 and KARD at 97 filling out the core tier. The score distribution is severely ceiling-pressed - all 44 signals land at or above 80 with a median of 94, which means the screener's dollar-size and seniority filters are doing the heavy lifting before scoring even begins, producing no real dispersion to rank within the tier. Macro sits NEUTRAL at 55.03, neither a tailwind nor a headwind, so these insider signals have to carry their own weight without regime support. JEF is the first name to track: most liquid of the three perfect-score names, a recognizable C-suite buy signal in financials, moderate conviction given the flat macro backdrop and absent pattern data.

🧠 Identifies individual laggards within momentum sectors, scoring each on sector relative strength times stock under-performance, targeting catch-up to the broader sector move.
Construction
Within LATE_CHASE / RIDING sectors, identifies the lagging individual stocks that haven't yet caught the sector move. Score = sector relative strength × stock under-performance.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

The score distribution is entirely flat: all 23 signals land at exactly 50, nothing clears 65, and no patterns are populated. SBAC and BEPC are the most structurally interesting names - a cell tower REIT and a renewable utility - but the screener gives them the same 50 as OPEN and ABR, so there is no differentiation to act on. The NEUTRAL macro regime at 55.03 is a marginal backdrop for catch-up plays, which need directional sector momentum rather than drift. Today's batch is too compressed to trade. If forced to prioritize, SBAC is the cleanest business model in the group, but conviction is low and the screener is not generating usable signal separation.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Ranks cheap non-financial, non-utility stocks by EV/EBIT to exploit mean reversion in the most operationally undervalued names in the investable universe.
Construction
Tobias Carlisle's Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT). Universe is market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services and Utilities (EV distorted), drops negative operating earnings. Ranks the universe by multiple ascending and surfaces the cheapest names (score 100 = cheapest of the day).

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $200M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (EV distorted)
  • Acquirer's Multiple = Enterprise Value / operating earnings (EBIT, operating income)
  • Operating earnings from Polygon financials (operating_income / EBIT); EV from yfinance else proxy market cap + non-current liabilities
  • Drops negative operating earnings; ranks the universe by multiple ascending (cheapest first)
Score
score = inverse rank scaled 0-100 (cheapest of the day = 100). LONG (deep value selection).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape (59% WR, avg +3.7% vs. SPY +0.6%) and the lone June cohort (57% WR, avg +5.1%) both confirm the screener is generating alpha, but the key anomaly is the Watch tier averaging +9.1% - outpacing the Very strong tier at +4.8% - which means score rank does not map cleanly to forward return rank.

BZ leads at score 100 (Very strong) and is the cleanest entry, with WRD at 97 and KROS at 93 rounding out a cluster of four Very strong names in the 90-100 band, though none carry a confirming pattern flag. The 16-signal batch is top-heavy: median score 73 with 7 names at 80 or above, meaning concentration sits in a narrow band rather than spreading broadly across the universe. A NEUTRAL macro regime (score 55) adds no directional tailwind for a slow-burn strategy, making timing uncertain even when the names are genuinely cheap. Take BZ first at high conviction given maximum score; size down on the Strong-tier names (GPC, PRE, NVAX) where 90-day tracking shows that tier dragging at a negative avg return of -1.6%.

The batch is weak. Five names tie at the ceiling score of 60 - CIFR, MARA, RIOT, PTF, and SBRA - but 60 sits below both the 65 and 80 thresholds, so nothing clears even the lower conviction bar. The crypto miner cluster (CIFR, MARA, RIOT) is the most coherent sub-theme today, implying BTC-correlated names are furthest along the Stage 2 setup, though all pattern fields are null and strip out the specific trigger detail. Score distribution is severely compressed: max 60, median 56 across 16 signals, with zero names above 65, meaning the tape is not generating high-conviction Stage 2 entries right now. NEUTRAL macro at 55 argues for sizing down rather than leaning in. If forced to act, MARA first among the miners, at low conviction only.

Data confidence Starter · untested · no rigorous backtest yet, queued for the untested-screeners battery. Methodology →
🧠 Flags SEC Form 4 cluster buys where 3 or more insiders purchase within 30 days, weighted by rank, dollar value, and recency.
Construction
SEC Form 4 cluster buys: 3+ insiders buying within 30 days, with C-suite or director rank weighted higher. Score uses cluster size, total $ value, insider rank, and recency.
Top picks: $BYRN $CLBK $CTM
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayBuysCXOClusterAvg value

11PRQR ↑50 Watch$1.79 -1.10%4✓·$247K
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • At least 1 insider buy on the ticker
  • Cluster if ≥ 3 unique insiders within a 14-day window
  • C-level detected if title is CEO/CFO/COO/CTO/President/Chairman
  • Average transaction-size tiers: ≥$200K or ≥$100K
Score
buy base (≥2 +15, 1 +5) + cluster +25 + C-level +20 + avg (≥200K +15, ≥100K +8), cap 60. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live tape supports keeping the screener active - 60% win rate and +5.0% avg 20-day return across 473 resolved trades beats SPY's -0.2% by 5.2 points, though the monthly cohort trend shows win rate slipping from 64% in May to 57% in June, and no rigorous backtest yet exists to confirm the edge is structural rather than regime-driven.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

Today's batch is flat and undifferentiated. All 11 signals score exactly 60, the median equals the max, zero names clear 65, and no pattern is flagged for any pick - the score distribution is fully compressed with no stack-rank to exploit. TSM is the highest-profile name in the group and carries the most liquidity, but it scores identically to micro-caps like FNWD and GLOO, which eliminates any scoring edge. The NEUTRAL macro regime (55.03) neither supports nor suppresses the thesis. With no score separation and no pattern confirmation, conviction is low across the board; if one name must go first, TSM at Watch-only given liquidity, not signal strength.

🧠 Ranks macro ETFs by Antonacci dual momentum, requiring positive 12-month absolute return and top relative rank among peers, then goes long the leaders.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors, scored on 20-day RS vs SPY, sector breadth, and cluster rank to identify rotation leaders.
Construction
Top relative-strength tickers within bullish sectors. Score = relative strength vs SPY (20d) + sector breadth + cluster ranking.
Top picks: $ABX $ACA $ACAD
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectorRet 1mRet 3m
1ABX ↑80●● Strong$10.05 -2.43%Financials18.7%30.6%
2ACA ↑68● Signal$145.11 -0.01%Industrials12.2%27.2%
3ACAD ↑64 Watch$24.83 -0.52%Healthcare18.9%13.9%
4ABVX ↑58 Watch$131.16 +1.10%Healthcare40.6%7.9%
5ACCO ↑57 Watch$3.92 -3.69%Industrials-2.5%25.5%
6AAMI ↑55 Watch$80.23 -2.54%Financials0.8%21.8%
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M
  • Step 1: bullish sectors = sector ETF (XLK/XLV/…) with 20d return > 0
  • Step 2: score the stocks in each bullish sector, top 10/sector
Score
Momentum 0-100 = 1M Ret (30%) + 3M Ret (30%) + RS vs SPY 3M (20%) + Volume Trend (20%), normalized components. ≥70 strong. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The 90-day tape confirms real edge - 57% win rate and +4.4% average over 180 resolved trades beats SPY by 6.1 points - and the June cohort's 100% win rate on 5 trades is directionally supportive, though the May best of 1149% distorts that month's +10.8% average and signals the screener can produce outlier-driven noise alongside genuine alpha.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

ACA at score 68 is the most actionable name today - it sits in the Signal tier, which historically runs 73% win rate and +9.4% average, outperforming every other tier including Very Strong and Strong by a wide margin. ABX tops the batch on raw score at 80 but lands in the Strong tier, which historically averages only +1.9% and a 49% win rate - score rank does not predict outcome here, tier does, so the ABX lead is misleading. The batch is thin at 6 names, median score 58, with only 2 clearing 65, and the distribution is clustered near the bottom of the scoring range rather than top-heavy in any meaningful way. Macro regime sits NEUTRAL at 55.03, providing no sector tailwind and capping conviction across the board. Take ACA first at moderate conviction, watch ABX only if tier data improves on the next refresh.

🧠 AI read · today's batch

All five names score 85 or 80, with the median sitting at 85 and every signal clearing the 80 threshold - this is an unusually tight, top-heavy batch with zero spread in the distribution. The most structurally interesting tension is SOXX (85, Strong, BULL) sitting alongside SOXS (85, Strong, BULL) simultaneously: a leveraged inverse semis ETF printing the same bullish flow score as its underlying-tracking counterpart is a contradiction worth pausing on, and could indicate positioning on both sides of a semis catalyst rather than a clean directional read. IWM (85, Strong, BULL) is the cleaner signal given a NEUTRAL macro regime at 55.03 - small caps responding to flow here is coherent with a mid-cycle tape without strong directional bias. GDX (80, Strong, BULL) adds a defensive layer that fits a NEUTRAL regime where gold flow tends to attract non-directional safe-haven bets. Pattern data is null across the board, which strips away one layer of confirmation. IWM is the first name given its lack of internal contradiction and regime fit, at moderate conviction given the SOXX/SOXS flag clouding the semis read.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=1 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Identifies pre-earnings straddles where implied vol underprices the Yang-Zhang HV20 expected move, targeting cheap optionality before a known catalyst.
Construction
Pre-earnings (3-14d to event) with straddle IV < expected move from Yang-Zhang HV20. Identifies when implied vol underprices realized expectation.
Top picks: $XOM $LYFT $ROKU
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySectionDTEExpectedPlay
1XOM ↑65● Signal$156.89 +1.58%PRE_IV_RAMP8—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
2LYFT ↑50 Watch$14.02 -4.37%PRE_IV_RAMP14—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
3ROKU ↑50 Watch$141.67 -0.99%PRE_IV_RAMP7—BUY CALL 30-45 DTE
4RTX ↓50 Watch$209.16 +7.33%REVERSAL0DOWN (reversal)BUY PUT (reversal)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Market cap ≥ $1B
  • PRE_IV_RAMP: earnings DTE 7-14d AND IV Rank < 50 (premium not yet expensive)
  • POST_PEAD: days_since earnings 0-5d
  • REVERSAL: earnings ≤ 2d AND pre-earnings drift ≥ 3%
Score
by section: PRE_IV_RAMP (DTE + IV rank + consistency + mcap, max 100), POST_PEAD (surprise + consistency + days + drift, max 100), REVERSAL (|drift| + DTE, max 60).
🧠 AI read · today's batch

XOM is the only name worth discussing: score 65, sole Signal-tier entry, LONG direction, though pattern data is absent so the thesis rests entirely on the vol-gap signal itself. LYFT and ROKU both land at 50 Watch with no pattern context to separate them, and RTX sits at the same floor as a SHORT Watch - three names at median with nothing to differentiate. The score distribution is compressed and bottom-heavy: four signals, median exactly 50, zero clearing 80, and only XOM above 65, which is a thin and uninspiring batch. A NEUTRAL macro regime at 55.03 does not actively work against vol-buying setups but provides no tailwind either. Take XOM first if anything, at low conviction only, and leave LYFT, ROKU, and RTX on the shelf until pattern data populates.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=25 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags tickers where IV30 exceeds HV20 by more than 1.5 standard deviations on a 90-day z-score, identifying outsized fear premiums as premium-selling candidates.
Construction
IV30 − HV20 spread spike on individual tickers (z-score > 1.5σ on 90d). Detects unusual fear premium - candidates for premium selling.
Top picks: $BMRN $IWM $BNTX
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30HV30RatioZone
1BMRN ↓83●● Strong$58.87 +0.80%49%23%2.09EXTREME_FEAR
2IWM ↓70● Signal$292.09 -0.58%27%15%1.76FEAR
3BNTX ↓66● Signal$92.06 +0.25%45%27%1.67FEAR
4ALNY ↓62 Watch$268.74 +0.66%58%37%1.56FEAR
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • ATM IV30 / realized HV30 ratio (price ≥ $10, ≥35 days of closes)
  • Zones: ≥2.0 EXTREME_FEAR, ≥1.5 FEAR, ≥1.2 ELEVATED, 1.0-1.2 NORMAL, <1.0 COMPLACENT
  • Archives only FEAR and EXTREME_FEAR
Score
min(100, ratio × 40) where ratio = IV30/HV30. NEUTRAL.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=3 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags names where put IV exceeds call IV at above-90th-percentile extremes, capturing unusual hedging demand as a contrarian long trigger.
Construction
Bearish IV skew bias: put IV >> call IV at extreme percentiles (>90th). Detects unusual put demand.
Top picks: $HON $LRCX $KLAC
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ daySkew ptsIV30IV rankVerdict
1HON ↑100●●● Very strong$246.27 +5.70%35.2934.1%78BEAR_SKEW_HIGH
2LRCX ↑70● Signal$319.78 +0.15%6.7194.1%91BEAR_SKEW_MILD
3KLAC ↑66● Signal$218.73 +1.88%5.4695.4%97BEAR_SKEW_MILD
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • 25Δ skew (30 DTE target) > +5 vol points (puts overpriced)
  • Earnings in 0-7 days (catalyst near)
  • IV Rank > 60 (high premium = fear)
  • Verdict: skew>10pts + IVRank>80 EXTREME, skew>7pts HIGH, else MILD
Score
clamp(50,100, 50 + skew_25d_pts × 3). LONG (BTFD contrarian: fear overpriced).
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No backtest exists to confirm or contradict (NOT_YET_BACKTESTED, needs N>=30), so the 90-day tape is the only evidence: nine resolved trades at 55.6% win rate and -5.70% average 20-day versus SPY at 0.77% is net negative in aggregate, June 2026 then reversed May's 75% win rate with a 0% win rate and -24.5% average, and the one coherent signal is the Very strong tier's +7.99% average on four trades, which is the number to watch as HON resolves.

🧠 Flags stocks with retail call-volume spikes, Reddit mention surges, and parabolic price action absent any earnings catalyst, targeting mean-reversion short entries.
Construction
Detects retail-driven euphoria: retail call volume spike, Reddit mention surge, parabolic price, no earnings catalyst. Score combines social attention z-score + call/put + price stretch.

Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • call_volume / call_open_interest ratio (euphoria contrarian)

Score

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects fake breakouts above the Value Area High that close back inside, trapping longs, and fires a short signal at end of day.
Construction
Fake breakout above VAH followed by a close back inside the value area = bull trap, longs trapped, SHORT signal.
Top picks: $AHCO $ACGL
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POC
1AHCO ↓65● Signal$10.47 -3.77%BULL_TRAP11.479.96
2ACGL ↓55 Watch$100.10 +1.49%BULL_TRAP101.0294.09
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BULL_TRAP (SHORT): close[N-1] > VAH then close[N] < VAH (failed bullish breakout rejected)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. SHORT.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=10 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Detects 25-delta put IV spikes vs the 90-day baseline, treating crowd hedging demand as a contrarian long trigger when the underlying is calm.
Construction
25-delta skew (put IV − call IV) spike vs 90d baseline. Bearish skew = hedging demand spike, contrarian read when stock is calm.
Top picks: $TSLA
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankSkew 25dPlaybook
1TSLA ↓100●●● Very strong$319.69 -14.52%298%97—SELL CALL/PUT (IV rich)
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Cross-section scan of the watchlist (large caps + ETFs)
  • Skew_Z = (skew_25d − basket median) / basket std
  • IV_Rich_Z = (IV_HV_spread − basket median) / basket std
  • Composite = IV_Rich_Z×1.0 + Skew_Z×0.5; archived if |Composite| ≥ 1.5
Score
int(|Composite| × 20). SHORT vol if Composite>0 (IV rich), LONG vol if <0.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 There is no validated backtest to compare against (N still below the 30-trade minimum), so the 90-day live tape (WR 51%, avg +3.6%, KEEP) is the only anchor, and the June 2026 cohort contradicts it sharply with WR 40% and avg -6.9% versus May's +10.1%, the key number being a 17-point win rate drop in one month that signals the edge is compressing.

Data confidence Starter · untested · INSUFFICIENT N=0 (battery 2026-06-09), needs N>=30 resolved trades. Methodology →
🧠 Flags value-area traps where price fakes a breakdown or breakout then reclaims within one bar, squeezing trapped shorts or longs.
Construction
Kerberos Apr-2020 thesis adapted to daily bars: a close below VAL (fake breakdown) followed by a reclaim next day = bear trap, shorts trapped, LONG signal. Mirror logic above VAH for bull traps.
Top picks: $ALH
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayTrap typeStopTarget POCATR
1ALH ↑60 Watch$25.17 +2.65%BEAR_TRAP23.9325.450.85
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • BEAR_TRAP (LONG): close[N-1] < VAL then close[N] > VAL (failed bearish breakout reclaimed)
  • fake_dist and reclaim_dist in [0.2, 2.0] ATR; total pct_move ≤ 12%
  • score ≥ 55 required
Score
50 base + volume_ratio (>1.5 +15) + fake_dist (>1.0 +10) + reclaim_dist (>0.5 +10) + rejection_wick (>0.5 +10) + cleanness (+5), bounded 0-100. LONG.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 No validated backtest exists (N=0 resolved trades in battery testing), so the 90-day live tape is the only evidence: 64.1% win rate and +4.9% average 20-day return across 78 resolved trades beats SPY's +0.1% by 4.7 points, though monthly cohorts show a softening from May (76.9% WR, +8.0% avg) to June (61.4%, +4.1%), suggesting the edge may be narrowing rather than compounding.

Last 90 days: 131 signals captured · 78 resolved at +20d · WR 64% · avg +4.9% · vs SPY +0.1%
Triple-barrier (TP=+3×ATR / SL=-2×ATR / max 20d): 96 closed · WR TB 44% · 18% expired
What this is: instead of forcing a fixed +20-day hold, triple-barrier simulates a realistic exit, take profit at +3×ATR, stop at -2×ATR, else time out at 20 days. So a name can read WIN here (target hit early) while its raw +20d return is still open or negative. It is the closer estimate of what you would actually have banked.

🧠 Detects volatility mispricing via IV rank, IV30/HV20 ratio, skew, term structure, and Kerberos-style flow in a composite R1 score.
Construction
Research-lab composite: IV rank + IV30/HV20 ratio + skew + term structure + Kerberos-style flow. Experimental scoring R1.
Top picks: $SVXY
#TickerScoreSignalPrice · Δ dayIV30IV rankHV20Rule
1SVXY ↓85●● Strong$56.34 -2.51%24%819%RULE_1
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Vol-decay basket (UVXY, VXX, SVXY, SDS, QID, SQQQ, FAZ, TZA)
  • Rule 1 (3 conditions required): IV30 < 100% AND IV Rank < 10 AND VIX3M/VIX > 1.0 (contango)
  • 2/3 conditions → inactive (score 55)
Score
if 3/3 active: min(100, 85 + max(0, 3−iv_rank)). NEUTRAL.
Performance — 90-day tape + 6-month cohorts
🧠 The live 90-day tape confirms the KILL verdict with no ambiguity: the key number is a 24.1% win rate and avg return of -8.8% against SPY's -0.4%, an 8.3-point underperformance, and the monthly cohorts from April through June show no improvement, printing averages of -24.6%, -9.1%, and -2.7% respectively.

Data confidence No backtest stats yet (N < 30 or not run) · starter size, discretionary read. Methodology & all-screeners comparison →
🧠 ALTMAN screens US equities using Z-score components to flag financial distress risk, surfacing names where deteriorating fundamentals may precede credit or equity stress.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filter: market cap > $300M, excludes Financial Services & Utilities (model not applicable)
  • Altman Z = 1.2×(WC/TA) + 1.4×(RE/TA) + 3.3×(EBIT/TA) + 0.6×(MktCap/TL) + 1.0×(Rev/TA)
  • Financials from Polygon (else yfinance)
  • Output filter: keeps only Z < 2.5 (grey + distress)
Score
score = the Z-Score itself. Zones: Z>2.99 SAFE, 1.81-2.99 GREY, <1.81 DISTRESS, <1.0 critical. LONG (risk filter).

  • Analyst gate: recommendationMean ≥ 3.0 (Hold or worse)
  • Fundamental gate: EVA Proxy Score ≤ 40 (low quality)
  • EVA Proxy 0-100 = 5 blocks of 20 pts: Profitability, Growth, Balance Sheet, Cash Flow, Valuation
Score
score = EVA Proxy (0-100, lower = worse). Top 50 sorted ascending (worst first). SHORT.

🧠 VSA flags price-spread and volume imbalances to identify institutional accumulation or distribution before directional follow-through.
Construction
Standard screener - refer to source script for full logic.
Top picks:
No signals ≥ score 50 for this date.
Exact criteria & score formula
Conditions checked
  • Universe filters: price > $3, 20d avg volume > 200K, market cap > $50M
  • Per-bar metrics (last 5): relative volume vs SMA20, normalized spread, close position within the range
  • Bullish patterns: No Supply, Testing, Stopping Volume, Absorption, Falling Pressure, Failed Down Move
  • Bearish patterns: No Demand, Up-Thrust, Buying Climax, Effort To Rise Failed
  • 20-bar accumulation/distribution background + Relative Strength vs SPY 20d
Score
best-pattern priority (No Supply 3 → Buying Climax 6) + background alignment (+2/-1) + RS vs SPY alignment (+1). Top 40. LONG or SHORT depending on pattern.

Not included on this page (by design)
These Google-Sheet tabs are not per-ticker screeners, so they have no card here: ETF_RANKING (asset-class ranking), CPR_RANK (Camarilla pivot assist), SECTOR_STRENGTH / SECTOR_BREADTH / SECTOR_ROTATION (sector-level gauges, see the macro cockpit), SKEW_VELOCITY / VOLATILITY_DASHBOARD / VVIX market panel (market-level vol gauges, see the cockpit), STRUCTURAL_FLOWS (OpEx / quarter-end calendar), QQQ_PAIRS / PAIR_ZSCORE (pair-trading lab, REJECT OOS), NEWS_SENTIMENT (newsletter prep data), MULTI_STRATEGY / META_SCREENER (aggregators, superseded by Best Trades), plus journal / portfolio / content tabs. Macro composites (BTFD index, COT, Kerberos PCR) live on the cockpit and macro pages.
Generated by build_screeners_deep.py · sources: signal_archive, whale_13f, openinsider, hrhr_ranked, options_flow_shortlist, emergence_history, sector_acceleration/laggards
Data confidence scale = a discretionary sizing guide (full / half / starter), never a permission verdict · stats from screener_validity + block-bootstrap v12
Sparklines = ~6 months daily close · AI reads regenerate at the EOD build, page refreshes hourly during US market hours
